notes: Marc Ostrom's only lead credit (2002). The summer at the mudflats · twelve walks, twelve species, one hearing. E1 was responsibility; E2 is WITNESS · you change nothing directly and it matters anyway, because standing at the hearing is made of looking.

shelf.shelf_condition: in a Betamax case · label pristine · Olaf's habit
shelf.prior_owner: Olaf, in the Betamax case Olaf preferred
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf kept the sticks in Betamax cases because he liked the size and because he had a shelf full of Betamax cases from the '80s that were doing nothing.
shelf.cover_blurb: One estuary.  Twelve months.  Eight species.  From Oneironautics Inc., the follow-up to ESTUARY (2001).  ESTUARY 2 is the calm one.  A soft simulation of a single Oregon-coast estuary through a full year.  You set tides.  You watch salmon.  You wait for the herons.  Recommended for players 10 and up.  Takes about four hours.
back_of_case: 'The one where nothing happens on purpose.'
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: Ines Rocha was NOT lead on Estuary 2. She was a junior on the team, doing per-species population math. Estuary 2's lead was Marc Ostrom, who would in 2003 be Rocha's most patient collaborator on the Estuary 3 Labor Day weekend build.

lore_tokens_conditional.estuary_2_hearing_dredged: the county votes for the marina
lore_tokens_conditional.estuary_2_hearing_blocked: the flat as you knew it · Ruth no longer waves
lore_tokens_conditional.e2_journal_<species>: one token per illuminated journal page (twelve possible)
